expectations.text: Explains lib.fakeHash as a bootstrap sentinel that fails to reveal the hash
expectations.evidence: 'a bootstrap sentinel that intentionally fails ... a sentinel, not a value you leave in place'

expectations.text: Warns committing fakeHash or vendorHash="" always fails
expectations.evidence: 'Never commit vendorHash = lib.fakeHash or vendorHash = "" ... Both always fail'

expectations.text: Shows subPackages = [ "cmd/mytool" ]
expectations.evidence: 'subPackages = [ "cmd/mytool" ];'

expectations.text: vendorHash = null only when no external deps
expectations.evidence: 'vendorHash = null ... only when the module has no external dependencies'

expectations.text: Avoids manual hash computation (fakeHash error-output is the workflow)
expectations.evidence: 'the failing build is the workflow'

expectations.text: Explains lib.fakeHash as a bootstrap sentinel that fails to reveal the hash
expectations.evidence: 'a throwaway sentinel ... aborts with a mismatch that prints the got: value'

expectations.text: Warns committing fakeHash or vendorHash="" always fails
expectations.evidence: 'Never commit lib.fakeHash and never use vendorHash = "" ... guaranteed to fail'

expectations.text: Shows subPackages = [ "cmd/mytool" ]
expectations.evidence: 'subPackages = [ "cmd/mytool" ];'

expectations.text: vendorHash = null only when no external deps
expectations.evidence: 'vendorHash = null is the correct value when the module has no external dependencies'

expectations.text: Avoids manual hash computation (fakeHash error-output is the workflow)
expectations.evidence: 'do not try to compute the hash by hand ... the error-output bootstrap is the intended path'

expectations.text: Uses --replace-fail; explains --replace silently succeeds on missing pattern
expectations.evidence: '--replace ... silently succeeds when the pattern is absent ... you ship a binary that still contains /usr/bin/curl'

expectations.text: Distinguishes ${curl} (Nix interpolation) from $out (shell var)
expectations.evidence: '${curl} is Nix interpolation ... $out, $CC ... are shell variables available only at build time'

expectations.text: Shows runHook preBuild and runHook postBuild in overridden buildPhase
expectations.evidence: 'runHook preBuild ... runHook postBuild'

expectations.text: Explains omitting runHook silently breaks dependency setup hooks
expectations.evidence: 'most common cause of mysterious build failures that appear after adding a dependency'

expectations.text: Prefers postPatch over patchPhase override to compose with other patches
expectations.evidence: 'postPatch is chosen over overriding patchPhase precisely so it stacks with patches'

expectations.text: Uses --replace-fail; explains --replace silently succeeds on missing pattern
expectations.evidence: 'Plain --replace succeeds silently even when the search string is absent ... baked into your binary'

expectations.text: Distinguishes ${curl} (Nix interpolation) from $out (shell var)
expectations.evidence: '${curl} is Nix string interpolation ... $out ... expanded at build time'

expectations.text: Shows runHook preBuild and runHook postBuild in overridden buildPhase
expectations.evidence: 'runHook preBuild ... runHook postBuild'

expectations.text: Explains omitting runHook silently breaks dependency setup hooks
expectations.evidence: 'classic it built fine until I added dependency X, now it fails mysteriously bug'

expectations.text: Prefers postPatch over patchPhase override to compose with other patches
expectations.evidence: 'Prefer postPatch over overriding patchPhase ... compose with declarative patches'

expectations.text: Places makeWrapper in nativeBuildInputs, not buildInputs
expectations.evidence: 'makeWrapper goes in nativeBuildInputs ... not a library linked into anything'

expectations.text: Uses lib.makeBinPath rather than manual store-path concatenation
expectations.evidence: 'lib.makeBinPath [ curl jq ] ... Hand-concatenating store paths is error-prone'

expectations.text: Explains wrapProgram embeds store paths; each PATH tool becomes a closure entry
expectations.evidence: 'hardcodes the store path of every tool ... becomes a runtime closure entry. This is the most common source of unexpected closure size'

expectations.text: Shows diagnostic sequence: file, strings | grep /nix/store/, nix why-depends
expectations.evidence: 'file /nix/store/... ; strings ... | grep /nix/store/ ; nix why-depends'

expectations.text: Avoids propagatedBuildInputs/buildInputs for shelled-out runtime tools
expectations.evidence: 'Do NOT use buildInputs or propagatedBuildInputs for tools you only shell out to'

notes: NON-DISCRIMINATING on all 3 evals (15/15 both configs). Baseline independently produced fakeHash-bootstrap semantics, vendorHash=null vs "", --replace-fail rationale, ${curl}-vs-$out split, runHook guards, makeWrapper-in-nativeBuildInputs, lib.makeBinPath, closure-growth explanation, and the file/strings/why-depends diagnostic sequence. Base model knows Nix packaging deeply.
